diagrams.title: Power/Interest Grid for Stakeholder Classification
diagrams.description: A 2x2 matrix classifying stakeholders based on their level of power (influence) and interest in the project outcomes
elements: High Power, High Interest (Top-Right): Manage Closely - Key players to actively engage
High Power, Low Interest (Top-Left): Keep Satisfied - Monitor but don't over-engage
Low Power, High Interest (Bottom-Right): Keep Informed - Regular communication
Low Power, Low Interest (Bottom-Left): Monitor - Minimal effort required
diagrams.purpose: Helps project managers prioritize stakeholder engagement strategies and allocate communication resources appropriately during the groundwork phase

diagrams.title: Requirements Traceability Matrix Structure
diagrams.description: A matrix showing relationships between requirements and downstream project artifacts
elements: Columns: Requirements ID, Description, Source, Design Components, Code Modules, Test Cases, Status
Rows: Individual requirements linked to corresponding artifacts
diagrams.purpose: Enables impact analysis when requirements change, ensures completeness, and supports validation and verification activities

diagrams.title: Scope Definition Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Visual representation of how project scope is defined from vision to specific deliverables
elements: Project Vision (Top): Overarching goal and purpose
Project Scope Statement (Middle): Boundaries, deliverables, exclusions, constraints
Work Breakdown Structure (Bottom): Detailed decomposition of deliverables into work packages
diagrams.purpose: Provides clear understanding of what is included and excluded in the project, preventing scope creep and stakeholder misunderstandings
